notes: Stage 3 (brief) and Stage 4 (checkpoint) are skippable when all issue mappings return high confidence — this condition is embedded in brief-1 and checkpoint-1 descriptions.
C3 (issue mapping halt) and C5/C6 (conventional commit format with issue number) are behavioral constraints embedded in generation and eval task descriptions, not separate tasks.

No agent with domain 'design' or 'product' was required — repo-orchestrator covers stages 2 and 5, project-orchestrator covers issue resolution in stage 2, repo-orchestrator owns stage 3 brief as closest domain fit.

tasks.subject: Workflow pre-flight: initialize run
tasks.description: Initialize the play run. Verify play is being invoked in a valid context. Set up STM paths under .meridian/{issue}/commit-code/. Load conventional commit types from LTM — do NOT hardcode them (C5).

tasks.subject: C1 branch guard: reject default branch
tasks.description: Check the current branch name. If the branch is main, master, or the repository's default branch (as reported by git), halt immediately with a clear message to the user. This check must occur before any file analysis. (Constraint C1)

tasks.subject: C2 changeset guard: exit gracefully if no changes
tasks.description: Check for the existence of staged, unstaged, and untracked files. If the changeset is empty, exit gracefully with a user-facing message explaining there is nothing to commit. This is not a failure condition — do not treat it as an error. (Constraint C2)

tasks.subject: C4 sensitive file scan: block on secrets or credentials
tasks.description: Scan all changed files (staged, unstaged, untracked) for sensitive file patterns: secrets, credentials, environment files (.env, .env.*), private keys, and certificate files. If any are detected, block the play and report the specific file paths and the reason each was flagged. Do not proceed until the files are removed from the changeset. (Constraint C4)

tasks.subject: Pre-flight gate: all guards passed
tasks.description: Confirm preflight-1, preflight-2, and preflight-3 all passed without halting. If any guard caused an early exit, this task is never reached. This is the gate that releases work into Stage 1.

tasks.subject: Intent resolution: load and validate intent
tasks.description: Read intent.yaml. Confirm all required fields are present (intent, constraints, failure_conditions, scenarios). Confirm the DAG has been resolved and written to STM. This task is owned by the intent-resolver and marks the transition from infrastructure setup to domain work.

tasks.subject: Analyze repository changes
tasks.description: Run analyze-changes skill. Collect all staged, unstaged, and untracked files. Group changes by concern (feature, fix, docs, config, test, etc.) using conventional commit type taxonomy loaded from LTM (C5). Produce a structured changeset artifact written to STM with: file list per group, proposed commit scope, grouping rationale, and confidence level per group. Failure condition check: flag any grouping where unrelated concerns appear in the same proposed commit (F1).

tasks.subject: Fetch open issues and resolve issue mappings
tasks.description: Run resolve-issues skill. Fetch all open issues from the project tracker. For each proposed commit group from STM (written by readiness-1), attempt to map the group to an existing issue. Record confidence level (high/low) per mapping. If any group cannot be mapped to an existing issue, halt and instruct the user to create the missing issue before continuing — do not proceed to generation with unmapped changes (C3). Write issue-mapping artifact to STM with: issue ID, title, mapping rationale, and confidence per commit group.

tasks.subject: Eval: validate changeset groupings
tasks.description: Evaluate the STM changeset artifact produced by readiness-1. Pass criteria: (1) every changed file appears in exactly one group, (2) no group contains files from unrelated concerns (F1 check), (3) each group has a stated grouping rationale. Fail criteria: any file appears in multiple groups, any group is missing a rationale, or mixed-concern groupings are detected. If this eval fails, halt and report the specific groups that failed before proceeding.

tasks.subject: Eval: validate issue mappings
tasks.description: Evaluate the STM issue-mapping artifact produced by readiness-2. Pass criteria: (1) every commit group has a mapped issue ID, (2) each mapping has a stated rationale, (3) confidence levels are recorded for all mappings. Fail criteria: any unmapped group exists (triggers C3 halt), any mapping references an issue that does not exist in the fetched open-issues list (F4 risk — flag for checkpoint review). If this eval fails with unmapped groups, halt immediately.

tasks.subject: Draft commit plan brief
tasks.description: Read STM artifacts from readiness-1 (changeset groups) and readiness-2 (issue mappings). Produce a human-readable commit plan brief showing: proposed commit groups, mapped issue per group with issue title, confidence level per mapping (high/low), and any flags requiring human review. This stage is skippable: if ALL issue mappings are high confidence and no flags exist, write a skip marker to STM and proceed directly to Stage 4 checkpoint (which will also skip). If any mapping is low confidence or flagged, the brief is required.

tasks.subject: Human checkpoint: approve or reject commit plan
tasks.description: Present the commit plan brief to the user. Output the brief from STM and wait for a typed response. Parse: Tether/tether → proceed to generation. Vanish/vanish → cancel and exit gracefully. Any other input → ask for clarification. This stage is skippable: if brief-1 wrote a skip marker (all mappings high confidence, no flags), bypass user presentation and proceed automatically to Stage 5. Do not use AskUserQuestion — output the brief and wait for typed response.

tasks.subject: Create commits from approved plan
tasks.description: Read the approved commit plan from STM (do not re-read the brief — read the structured STM artifacts directly). For each commit group: (1) stage the files in that group, (2) construct a conventional commit message using the type from LTM taxonomy (C5), a scope derived from the change grouping, a description that specifically describes what was changed — not a generic placeholder (F2 check), and the linked issue number in the message body or footer (C6). Execute git commit. Repeat for each group in dependency order. All changed files must be committed — no file may be left uncommitted without a documented reason (F3 check).

tasks.subject: Eval: validate produced commits
tasks.description: Evaluate the commits produced by gen-1 against all failure conditions. Check F1: no commit contains files from unrelated concerns — verify each commit's file list matches its stated grouping. Check F2: every commit message describes the actual change, not a generic placeholder — reject any message of the form 'fix: resolve issue #N' with no substantive description. Check F3: run git status — confirm no changed files remain uncommitted, and if any do, confirm a documented reason exists in STM. Check F4: verify each commit's referenced issue ID matches the issue mapping in STM — flag any mismatch. If any check fails, report the specific commit(s) and failure reason. Do not proceed to scenario validation if F1, F2, or F3 failures exist.

tasks.subject: S1: code reviewer readability validation
tasks.description: Scenario S1 — Persona: Code reviewer. Given: the git log of commits produced by this play on a feature branch. Evaluate: can a code reviewer understand the scope and nature of each change without reading the diff, and can they trace every commit to its originating issue? Pass criteria: (1) every commit message contains a substantive description of the change, (2) every commit message references an issue number, (3) the set of commits covers all changed files with no unexplained gaps. Fail criteria: any commit message that requires reading the diff to understand the change, or any commit with no traceable issue reference.

tasks.subject: S2: team lead progress and mentoring validation
tasks.description: Scenario S2 — Persona: Team lead. Given: the git log of commits produced by this play across multiple branches. Evaluate: can a team lead report on progress per issue using commit history, and can they use the commit history as a mentoring reference for conventional commit practices? Pass criteria: (1) commits are grouped per issue such that per-issue progress is traceable, (2) all commit messages follow conventional commit format (type, scope, description) as defined by LTM taxonomy (C5), (3) the commit history as a whole demonstrates consistent conventional commit practice with no deviations. Fail criteria: commits that cannot be attributed to a specific issue, or commit messages that deviate from conventional format.

tasks.subject: Evidence and close: record run outcome
tasks.description: Collect and persist run evidence to STM at .meridian/{issue}/commit-code/evidence/. Write: (1) final git log of all commits produced, (2) issue-mapping artifact from readiness-2, (3) scenario validation results from scenario-1 and scenario-2 (pass/fail per criterion), (4) any constraint violations encountered and how they were resolved (or that they caused a halt). Mark the play run as complete. If any scenario failed, mark the run as failed with the specific failure reasons.
